data.title: As a DSO developer, I can assess ticket description quality deterministically
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Create ticket-clarity-check.sh — a deterministic heuristic script that scores ticket description quality using type-conditional weighted signals.
**Why**: Layer 1 of the 3-layer clarity gate. Provides a fast, API-free first pass before falling through to haiku classification.
**Scope**:
- IN: New script with type-conditional scoring (tasks, stories, bugs, epics each have specific bonus signals), configurable threshold via dso-config.conf, exit 0 (pass) / exit 1 (fail) with score on stdout
- OUT: Integration into sprint entry (Story 3), threshold calibration (Story 5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, ticket-clarity-check.sh accepts a ticket ID as argument, retrieves the ticket description via .claude/scripts/dso ticket show, and returns exit 0 (pass) or exit 1 (fail) with the numeric score on stdout
  ← Satisfies: "A deterministic heuristic script (ticket-clarity-check.sh) scores ticket description quality"
- When this story is complete, the script applies type-conditional weighted signals: section headers (+1), description length >=200 chars (+1) / >=500 chars (+1), bullet/checkbox lists (+1), plus type-specific bonuses as specified in SC2
  ← Satisfies: "using type-conditional weighted signals"
- When this story is complete, the pass threshold defaults to 5 and is configurable via clarity_check.pass_threshold in dso-config.conf with a minimum valid value of 1
  ← Satisfies: "Default pass threshold: 5 (configurable via clarity_check.pass_threshold)"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] When the script is absent on existing installations, sprint (Story 3) treats it as a heuristic failure — the script itself does not need to handle this case
- [Interface] Script accepts a ticket ID as its sole argument and retrieves the ticket description internally via .claude/scripts/dso ticket show — this is the integration contract Story 3 depends on
